question_text: A loudspeaker produces two different sound waves. Wave A has a frequency of 440 Hz and a wavelength of 0.78 m. Wave B has a frequency of 880 Hz and a wavelength of 0.39 m. Calculate the wave speed for both waves and explain what your results tell you about how sound travels through air.
correct_answer: Wave A: 343.2 m/s; Wave B: 343.2 m/s. Both waves travel at the same speed because sound travels at a constant speed through the same medium (air), regardless of frequency or wavelength.
explanation: Using wave speed = frequency × wavelength, both calculations give approximately 343 m/s, which is the actual speed of sound in air. This shows that when frequency doubles, wavelength halves to keep the wave speed constant in the same material.
